abstract: This article examines the reform of Polish water law, focusing on the fundamental objectives behind the proposed new Water Law Act. Key areas of analysis include the reorganization of administrative competencies, the separation of infrastructure management from water resource management, and the implementation of a catchment-oriented policy and simplified financing models. By conducting a comparative study of existing regulations and proposed legislative changes, the authors highlight systemic flaws within the current legal framework and identify their root causes. Ultimately, the paper seeks to evaluate whether the proposed legal solutions offer a genuine remedy to the persistent challenges facing the national water management system.
